description: matchAuthToken uses strict equality (===); matchUiLabel and matchFeatureToggle use loose equality (==) which performs type coercion.

findings.rationale: matchAuthToken uses === while the other two matchers use ==. The strict-equality choice is load-bearing on the auth path: with ==, an attacker who can coerce one side to a falsy number ("" == 0, "0" == false) bypasses the comparison. Unifying onto a shared compare() that uses == re-introduces an auth-bypass vector.
